title: Census transformation research programme | Stats NZ
content:
## Census transformation research programme
The Census transformation research programme informs the methodological development of census into the future.

## Background and history of the Census transformation research programme
The Census transformation research programme was established to progress research into alternative ways of producing demographic, social, and economic statistics covering small area populations. It followed the publication of Transforming the New Zealand Census of Population and Dwellings: Issues, options, and strategy in 2012, which set out a plan for transforming the census, proposing a short-term focus on modernising the current census model, with a long-term goal to develop a new census model based on administrative data.
The 2015 Cabinet paper, Census transformation – A promising future agreed that working towards a future census based primarily on administrative data, supported by redevelopment of its household surveys, was a promising direction for the New Zealand census.

## Use of administrative data in the census
Results from census transformation research allowed us to use administrative data to help compensate for the lower-than-expected participation in the 2018 Census. See Overview of statistical methods for adding admin records to the 2018 Census dataset for more information.
Using a combined census model for the 2023 Census sets out the methodologies of the 2023 Census model. Administrative data, data from previous censuses, and statistical imputation was used to fill gaps in census variables and add records to the census file. In 2023, completing a census form was the main way information was collected, and administrative data was only used where the information had not already been captured through census form responses.

## The Experimental administrative population census
An important output of the census transformation research programme has been the development of the Experimental administrative population census (APC) . This product builds on research across the programme to derive census-type information from linked administrative data. The data are not official statistics and are published to demonstrate the information currently available. It provides a focus for discussion about the benefits and limitations associated with a census based on administrative data. The APC is currently in its third iteration, with each iteration building on the previous one to deliver a wider range of attributes and improved methodology.
The APC does not provide all the information currently available from the census: information such as languages spoken or religious affiliation, for example, are not available in administrative data. Any administrative-first census model will require surveying of the population to support information needs not able to be met through administrative data.
Papers exploring the potential for administrative sources to provide census-type information for a range of topics are available below.
For more information please contact us: censustransformation@stats.govt.nz

## Census transformation publications

## Creating household and family information from admin data: Progress, limitations, and next census
Provides an update of our current understanding of how well administrative (admin) data can produce household and family statistics, compared with past censuses.

## Iwi affiliation in admin and historical census data
Provides an evaluation of the current availability and quality of administrative (admin) and historical census data sources within the Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI) for iwi affiliation.

## Technical feasibility: Measuring population and dwellings using administrative data (2025 update)
Provides an update on the technical feasibility of using administrative (admin) data, supported by surveys, to measure New Zealand’s population and dwellings in the next census.

## Quality of ethnicity data in the experimental administrative population census (APC): High level summary
Assesses the quality of ethnicity data in the Experimental administrative population census (APC) compared with ethnicity data collected in the 2023 Census.

## Assessing the quality of partnership status attributes from admin data
Describes the method for deriving the two census attributes ‘Legally registered relationship status’ and ‘Partnership status in current relationship’ from admin data, and assesses the quality of the resulting attributes.

## Reviewing the quality of ethnicity data from administrative sources: Comparison with the census
Provides an assessment of the current quality of administrative (admin) data for deriving the ethnicity of the New Zealand population.

## Technical feasibility: Measuring population and dwellings using administrative data
Summarises work investigating the technical feasibility of transforming the New Zealand census to a model based primarily on administrative (admin) data supported by surveys.

## Finding the best source of coverage for state social housing: Comparing Kāinga Ora (Housing New Zealand) and census data
Explores the potential of administrative (admin) sources to provide census type information for estimating the dwelling stock of state social housing in New Zealand. We focus on understanding data from the 2013 and 2018 Censuses.

## Experimental administrative population census (third iteration): Data sources, methods, and quality for household information
Describes the data and methods used to compile the first release of the experimental administrative household data and provides information about the quality of the data.

## Experimental administrative population census

## Estimating population size of four ethnic groupings in New Zealand
Research paper reporting on a method for estimating populations by ethnicity, using multiple system estimation and latent class analysis.

## Estimating income from linked admin data: Impact of new sources
An evaluation of the quality of admin-sourced income data for total personal income, and sources of personal income, the two main personal income measures in the census.

## Harmonising ethnicity from multiple administrative data sources using latent class modelling
Describes a method for predicting an individual’s ethnic group profile using all ethnicity data available from multiple sources by applying a classification procedure known as latent class modelling.

## Experimental administrative population census:  Data sources and methods
Describes a new experimental approach for deriving census information from administrative (admin) data.

## Administrative sources for census housing information: An overview
One of a series of investigations by the census transformation programme identifying and exploring the potential for administrative data sources to provide census-type information.

## The quality of administrative data for census variables: Strengths, limitations, and opportunities
Summarises the census transformation programme research to date on the quality of census attribute information derived from administrative (admin) data sources.

## Comparing labour force variables from 2013 Census and administrative data
Explores the potential for administrative data to provide information for the two main labour market measures in the census: work and labour force status and status in employment.

## Potential for admin data to provide country of birth and years since arrival in New Zealand information
Explores the potential for admin sources to provide census-type information about the country of birth and years since arrival in New Zealand.

## Comparing 2013 Census and admin data for number of children born
Presents our investigation into the potential for deriving information on the number of children born from admin data.

## Experimental population estimates from linked administrative data
View experimental population estimates for New Zealand derived from linked administrative data in the Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI).

## The potential for linked administrative data to provide household and family information
One of a series of investigations by the Census Transformation programme identifying and exploring the potential for administrative data sources to provide census-type information.

## Comparing housing information from census and tenancy bond data
Compares information about ‘weekly rent paid’, ‘sector of landlord’, and ‘number of bedrooms’ from the 2013 Census with estimates produced from the tenancy bond data available in Stats NZ’s Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI).

## Experimental population estimates from linked administrative data: 2017 release
An update on our research into estimating national and subnational populations using administrative data. Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## Comparing income information from census and administrative sources
Describes our preliminary analysis of income information available from administrative sources. Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## Experimental population estimates from linked administrative data: methods and results
Describes a method used to produce national population estimates by age and sex using administrative data. Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## Comparison of ethnicity information in administrative data and the census
Outlines enduring census information requirements for and about Māori. Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## Identifying Māori populations using administrative data: A comparison with the census
Investigates using administrative sources to provide essential information for and about Māori. Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## Identifying the New Zealand resident population in the Integrated Data Infrastructure
Describes a method for determining who is a resident in NZ  using the linked administrative data sources in the Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI). Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## Enduring census information requirements for and about Māori
Outlines enduring census information requirements for and about Māori. Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## Quality of geographic information in the Integrated Data Infrastructure
Examines the quality of location information in the administrative data sources in the Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI) compared with the geographic information in the 2013 Census. Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## Comparing education and training information in administrative data sources and census
Compares results from the 2013 Census with estimates produced from administrative sources for ‘highest qualification’ and ‘study participation’. Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## Quality standards for population statistics: Accuracy requirements for future census models
Aims to answer the question: what does ‘fit for purpose’ mean for population statistics now and into the future? Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## Coverage assessment in an administrative census: A progress report on issues and methods
Reports on preliminary investigation into issues and methods for coverage assessment, if Stats NZ moves to a census based primarily on administrative data. Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## Evaluating the potential of linked data sources for population estimates: The Integrated Data Infrastructure as an example
Describes an investigation into using linked administrative data to produce a statistical population list. Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## An initial investigation into the potential for administrative data to provide census long-form information
A broad look at the potential for administrative data to produce the long-form (social and economic) information currently provided by the census. Part of our Census Transformation programme.
Quantifies in dollar terms the benefits to New Zealand gained through the use of census and population statistics information. Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## An overview of progress on the potential use of administrative data for census information in New Zealand: Census Transformation programme
Provides an overview of early investigations into the potential use of administrative data sources for census information. Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## Options for future New Zealand censuses: Census Transformation programme
Outlines potential options for future censuses in New Zealand. Part of our Census Transformation programme.

## Transforming the New Zealand Census of Population and Dwellings: Issues, options, and strategy
Presents issues associated with transforming the New ZealandCensus of Population and Dwellings. It proposes a strategic direction and work programme for the transformation.

## Future New Zealand censuses: Implications of changing census frequency or adopting other models
Informs discussion about the potential for adopting different approaches to census-taking in New Zealand.

## A register-based census: what is the potential for New Zealand?
Describes the changes that would be needed in New Zealand to replace the traditional census with a register-based census.
